tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and very low HbA1c, suggesting possible overtreatment of diabetes or malnutrition. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urine findings (proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, abnormal color/appearance) suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation, review of diabetes management, and nephrology evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
